# local-agent

> A local-first coding agent for the terminal, built in Go with Charm and powered by Ollama.

local-agent is alpha software for macOS and Linux. It can inspect repositories,
edit files with approval, run tools, connect MCP servers, resume local sessions,
and execute reviewed durable goals. It is not an operating-system sandbox.

## Documentation

- [Getting started](https://local-agent.dev/getting-started): Install Ollama and local-agent, then begin a local coding session.
- [Ollama models](https://local-agent.dev/ollama-models): Live inventory, local and cloud policy, routing, pinning, and model admission.
- [Modes and goals](https://local-agent.dev/modes-and-goals): Normal, Plan, Auto, authority boundaries, budgets, and stop conditions.
- [Expert teams](https://local-agent.dev/experts): Read-only Team, Swarm, and application-level MoE consultations with machine-adaptive concurrency.
- [Configuration](https://local-agent.dev/configuration): Models, privacy, tools, MCP servers, profiles, memory, and terminal behavior.
- [Command reference](https://local-agent.dev/reference): CLI commands, slash commands, keyboard shortcuts, and automation boundaries.
- [MCP integrations](https://local-agent.dev/mcp): Supported transports, namespaced tools, approvals, and bounded receipts.
- [Sessions and memory](https://local-agent.dev/sessions-and-memory): SQLite resume, checkpoints, reasoning, and optional retrieval.
- [Safety and privacy](https://local-agent.dev/safety): Approval gates, workspace and temporary typed external-path boundaries, local-only defaults, and known limitations.
- [Testing](https://local-agent.dev/testing): Go verification and Glyphrun terminal behavior specs.
- [Architecture](https://local-agent.dev/architecture): Agent loop, model manager, tool policy, MCP registry, storage, goals, and UI.
- [Ecosystem](https://local-agent.dev/ecosystem): Related local-first developer tools and evidence systems.

## Project
